zh.lifeSummary: 當Bolko "Bolesław" "The Strict" of Poland I於1253年7月17日出生時，他的父親Bolesław II Rogatka Piast29歲，母親Hedwig von Anhalt34歲。他和Beatrix de Brandenburg-Mecklenburg Heiress of Stargard至少有3個兒子和5個女兒。他去世於1301年11月9日在波蘭多爾諾希利a斯吉萊格尼尼次吉享年48歲，埋葬在波蘭索福斯吉波維亞特 萊札斯吉可頌福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Bolko "Bolesław" "The Strict" of Poland I于1253年7月17日出生时，他的父亲Bolesław II Rogatka Piast29岁，母亲Hedwig von Anhalt34岁。他和Beatrix de Brandenburg-Mecklenburg Heiress of Stargard至少有3个儿子和5个女儿。他去世于1301年11月9日在波兰多爾諾希利a斯吉萊格尼尼次吉享年48岁，埋葬在波兰索福斯吉波維亞特 萊札斯吉可頌福。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Poland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Hampshire called Poland, which probably derives from Old English Pōling or Pulling ‘pool place’. Alternatively, it may be a variant of any of the similar (like-sounding) English surnames, such as Pulham and Pullen , altered by folk etymology to conform to the name of the country in central Europe.
Irish (County Offaly, also Armagh and Down): variant of   Polin  . Compare   Polan  .
German: ethnic name from Middle High German Polan ‘Poland’ + excrescent -d, denoting an ethnic Pole or someone with Polish connections.

variants.name: Poling
meanings: Probably from German habitational name from Polling (Bavaria, Austria).

English (Sussex and Hampshire) and Irish: variant of either Poland or Powling, either a habitational name from Poling in Sussex, or a nickname from an unrecorded Middle English polling, a derivative of Middle English polle ‘head, hair of the head’ or pollen ‘to cut (hair), shave (someone's head)’ + the noun-forming suffix -ing. It may have been given to someone with a distinctive head of hair or who had a shaven head.

variants.name: Polland
meanings: English (southwest Scotland): variant of   Poland  .
German: variant of   Poland   or   Pollan  .

variants.name: Pulham
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places called Pulham, in Norfolk, Dorset, and Devon. The placenames derive from Old English pōl, pull ‘pool’ + hām ‘village, homestead’ or hamm ‘water meadow’.

variants.name: Pollins
meanings: English, Irish and Scottish: variant of Poland , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Bolko
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Belorussian: nickname for a fair-haired or pale-skinned man, derived from bely ‘white, light, fair’ + the noun suffix -ko.

Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian: nickname with the same meaning as above, from derivatives and dialect forms of Slovak biely and Croatian bijel, or from Slovenian bel. It may also be from a pet form of the Old Slavic personal names, such as Belimir (see Belic ). This surname is also found in Hungary, where it is spelled Belkó.

variants.name: Belic
meanings: Serbian and Croatian (Belić); Slovenian (Belič): nickname for a fair-skinned or fair-haired man, derived from Serbian beo, Croatian bijel, Slovenian bel ‘white, light, fair’, or a patronymic from the nickname Beli, with the same meaning; or a patronymic (metronymic) from a short form of an old compound personal name based on Old Slavic bělъ with the same meaning, e.g. Belimir. Compare Belich and Bellich , see also Bilic .

nameMeanings.name: "The
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a variant spelling of Ty or a transferred use of the surname, a local name for someone who lived by a common pasture, Middle English tye.

nameMeanings.name: Strict"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German:
metonymic occupational name for a rope maker, from Middle High German stric, Middle Low German strik ‘cord, rope’.
nickname for a rogue or prankster.

en.lifeSummary: When Bolko "Bolesław" "The Strict" of Poland I was born on 17 July 1253, his father, Bolesław II Rogatka Piast, was 29 and his mother, Hedwig von Anhalt, was 34. He had at least 3 sons and 5 daughters with Beatrix de Brandenburg-Mecklenburg Heiress of Stargard. He died on 9 November 1301, in Legnica, Lower Silesia, Poland, at the age of 48, and was buried in Krzeszów, Leżajsk County, Rzeszów, Poland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Bolko "Bolesław" "The Strict" of Poland I est né le 17 juillet 1253, son père, Bolesław II Rogatka Piast, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Hedwig von Anhalt, avait 34 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles avec Beatrix de Brandenburg-Mecklenburg Heiress of Stargard. Il est décédé le 9 novembre 1301, à Legnicki, dolnośląskie, Pologne, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterré à Krzeszów, Powiat leżajski, rzeszowskie, Pologne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Bolko "Bolesław" "The Strict" of Poland I am 17. Juli 1253 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Bolesław II Rogatka Piast 29 und seine Mutter Hedwig von Anhalt 34. Er hatte mit Beatrix de Brandenburg-Mecklenburg Heiress of Stargard mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 9. November 1301 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Legnicki, Niederschlesien, Polen und wurde in Krzeszów, Powiat leżajski, rzeszowskie, Polen begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Bolko "Bolesław" "The Strict" of Poland I è nato il il 17 luglio 1253, il suo padre, Bolesław II Rogatka Piast, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Hedwig von Anhalt, aveva 34 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie con Beatrix de Brandenburg-Mecklenburg Heiress of Stargard. Morì il 9 novembre 1301, nel Legnicki, dolnośląskie, Polonia, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolto a Krzeszów, Powiat leżajski, rzeszowskie, Polonia.

ja.lifeSummary: Bolko "Bolesław" "The Strict" of Poland Iが1253年7月17日に生まれた時，父Bolesław II Rogatka Piastは29歳，母Hedwig von Anhaltは34歳であった。彼はBeatrix de Brandenburg-Mecklenburg Heiress of Stargardとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼は1301年11月9日にポーランドドルノsルaスキレグニクイで48歳で死去し，ポーランドルズエスゾウスキポウィアト レzアジスキクルズエスズoウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Bolko "Bolesław" "The Strict" of Poland I(이)가 1253년 7월 17일에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Bolesław II Rogatka Piast(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Hedwig von Anhalt(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Beatrix de Brandenburg-Mecklenburg Heiress of Stargard(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1301년 11월 9일 폴란드 도노sa스키 레그닉키에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 폴란드 르즈스즈우스키 파위앳 레z아제이스키 크르즈스즈ow에 묻혔다.

Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas com Beatrix de Brandenburg-Mecklenburg Heiress of Stargard. Ele faleceu em 9 de novembro de 1301, em Legnicki, dolnośląskie, Polônia, com 48 anos, e foi sepultado em Krzeszów, Powiat leżajski, rzeszowskie, Polônia.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bolko "Bolesław" "The Strict" of Poland I родился 17 июля 1253 г., его отцу по имени Bolesław II Rogatka Piast было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Hedwig von Anhalt, 34. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей от Beatrix de Brandenburg-Mecklenburg Heiress of Stargard. Он умер 9 ноября 1301 г. в местности легникки, долноsлaски, Польша в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности крзесзoв, поуят леzаджски, рзесзоуски, Польша.
